1	// Package triage provides the post-attempt decision module that the
2	// drain loop consults after each terminal event. Given a bead's failure
3	// mode and the prior modes seen in this bead's attempt history, the
4	// policy returns the next action to take (re-attempt, escalate, file
5	// follow-up, etc.).
6	//
7	// This package is the standalone policy core; loop integration lands in
8	// a follow-up bead.
9	package triage
10	
11	import "fmt"
12	
13	// FailureMode classifies the terminal event of a single execute-bead attempt.
14	type FailureMode string
15	
16	const (
17		// FailureModeReviewBlock — reviewer returned BLOCK on the committed result.
18		FailureModeReviewBlock FailureMode = "review_block"
19		// FailureModeLockContention — claim/lock could not be acquired.
20		FailureModeLockContention FailureMode = "lock_contention"
21		// FailureModeExecutionFailed — runner crashed, harness errored, or
22		// timed out without producing a usable commit.
23		FailureModeExecutionFailed FailureMode = "execution_failed"
24		// FailureModeNoChanges — attempt completed without producing a commit.
25		FailureModeNoChanges FailureMode = "no_changes"
26	)
27	
28	// Action is the decision the policy returns for an attempt.
29	type Action string
30	
31	const (
32		// ActionReAttemptWithContext — re-queue the bead with the prior
33		// failure context threaded into the next prompt.
34		ActionReAttemptWithContext Action = "re_attempt_with_context"
35		// ActionEscalateTier — re-queue with a higher-capability model tier.
36		ActionEscalateTier Action = "escalate_tier"
37		// ActionRetryWithBackoff — retry after a delay (lock contention etc.).
38		ActionRetryWithBackoff Action = "retry_with_backoff"
39		// ActionFileFollowup — file a follow-up bead and close this one.
40		ActionFileFollowup Action = "file_followup"
41		// ActionNeedsHuman — park the bead for manual triage.
42		ActionNeedsHuman Action = "needs_human"
43	)
44	
45	// TriagePolicy maps a FailureMode to an ordered ladder of Actions. The
46	// nth time a given mode is seen in a bead's history, the nth rung of
47	// its ladder is returned. Past the end of the ladder, the final rung
48	// (typically ActionNeedsHuman) is returned.
49	type TriagePolicy struct {
50		Ladders map[FailureMode][]Action
51	}
52	
53	// DefaultPolicy returns the built-in policy. Mirrors the parent bead's
54	// AC#3: BLOCK → re_attempt_with_context, escalate_tier, needs_human.
55	func DefaultPolicy() TriagePolicy {
56		return TriagePolicy{Ladders: map[FailureMode][]Action{
57			FailureModeReviewBlock: {
58				ActionReAttemptWithContext,
59				ActionEscalateTier,
60				ActionNeedsHuman,
61			},
62			FailureModeLockContention: {
63				ActionRetryWithBackoff,
64				ActionRetryWithBackoff,
65				ActionNeedsHuman,
66			},
67			FailureModeExecutionFailed: {
68				ActionReAttemptWithContext,
69				ActionEscalateTier,
70				ActionNeedsHuman,
71			},
72			FailureModeNoChanges: {
73				ActionReAttemptWithContext,
74				ActionFileFollowup,
75				ActionNeedsHuman,
76			},
77		}}
78	}
79	
80	// Decide returns the next Action for an attempt that ended in `mode`,
81	// given the ordered list of failure modes already seen for this bead
82	// (most recent last; the current attempt is NOT included).
83	//
84	// beadID is accepted for future per-bead policy customisation and to
85	// keep call sites stable; the default policy ignores it.
86	func (p TriagePolicy) Decide(beadID string, mode FailureMode, history []FailureMode) Action {
87		ladder := p.Ladders[mode]
88		if len(ladder) == 0 {
89			return ActionNeedsHuman
90		}
91		n := 0
92		for _, m := range history {
93			if m == mode {
94				n++
95			}
96		}
97		if n >= len(ladder) {
98			return ladder[len(ladder)-1]
99		}
100		return ladder[n]
101	}
102	
103	// ParseAction validates and converts a string from config into an Action.
104	func ParseAction(s string) (Action, error) {
105		switch Action(s) {
106		case ActionReAttemptWithContext,
107			ActionEscalateTier,
108			ActionRetryWithBackoff,
109			ActionFileFollowup,
110			ActionNeedsHuman:
111			return Action(s), nil
112		default:
113			return "", fmt.Errorf("triage: unknown action %q", s)
114		}
115	}
116	
117	// ParseFailureMode validates and converts a string from config into a FailureMode.
118	func ParseFailureMode(s string) (FailureMode, error) {
119		switch FailureMode(s) {
120		case FailureModeReviewBlock,
121			FailureModeLockContention,
122			FailureModeExecutionFailed,
123			FailureModeNoChanges:
124			return FailureMode(s), nil
125		default:
126			return "", fmt.Errorf("triage: unknown failure mode %q", s)
127		}
128	}
129

1	package agent
2	
3	// Triage dispatch wires post-attempt failure modes to the standalone
4	// triage.TriagePolicy. It covers three integration paths described by the
5	// parent bead's AC:
6	//
7	//   - lock_contention: classify staging-tracker / .git/index.lock errors
8	//     and retry the operation with a 3-step backoff before escalating.
9	//   - execution_failed: consult the policy, which advances escalate_tier →
10	//     needs_human as attempts accumulate.
11	//   - no_changes: when the worker's rationale does not cite the bead's AC,
12	//     file a clarification follow-up bead and close the original.
13	
14	import (
15		"context"
16		"encoding/json"
17		"fmt"
18		"strings"
19		"time"
20	
21		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
22		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/triage"
23	)
24	
25	// LockContentionBackoff is the default 3-step backoff schedule applied when
26	// an attempt fails with a lock-contention signal. Total worst-case wait per
27	// attempt = 30s + 90s + 300s = 7m. Tests override this by passing their own
28	// schedule into RunWithLockBackoff.
29	var LockContentionBackoff = []time.Duration{
30		30 * time.Second,
31		90 * time.Second,
32		300 * time.Second,
33	}
34	
35	// IsLockContentionError reports whether errMsg names a transient lock-acquire
36	// failure that the dispatcher should retry. Patterns cover both git's index
37	// lock and ddx's own staging/tracker locks.
38	func IsLockContentionError(errMsg string) bool {
39		lower := strings.ToLower(errMsg)
40		return containsAny(lower,
41			".git/index.lock",
42			"unable to create '.git/index.lock'",
43			"another git process seems to be running",
44			"index.lock: file exists",
45			"staging-tracker lock",
46			"tracker lock held",
47			"tracker is locked",
48		)
49	}
50	
51	// ClassifyAttemptForTriage maps an ExecuteBeadReport to the triage.FailureMode
52	// that the dispatcher should consult. Returns "" when the report does not
53	// belong to any of the modes this bead wires (success, push_failed, etc.).
54	func ClassifyAttemptForTriage(report ExecuteBeadReport) triage.FailureMode {
55		switch report.Status {
56		case ExecuteBeadStatusReviewBlock:
57			return triage.FailureModeReviewBlock
58		case ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges:
59			return triage.FailureModeNoChanges
60		case ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed:
61			if IsLockContentionError(report.Detail) {
62				return triage.FailureModeLockContention
63			}
64			return triage.FailureModeExecutionFailed
65		}
66		return ""
67	}
68	
69	// RationaleCitesAC returns true when a no-changes rationale references the
70	// bead's acceptance criteria with enough specificity to treat the bead as
71	// already satisfied. The check passes when:
72	//   - rationaleIsSpecific (already used by adjudicateNoChanges) holds, OR
73	//   - the rationale shares at least 2 distinct content tokens (lower-cased
74	//     alphanumeric, length ≥ 6) with the acceptance text.
75	//
76	// A vague rationale ("nothing to do", "already done") fails both checks and
77	// is treated as a clarification request, prompting a follow-up bead.
78	func RationaleCitesAC(rationale, acceptance string) bool {
79		if strings.TrimSpace(rationale) == "" {
80			return false
81		}
82		if rationaleIsSpecific(rationale) {
83			return true
84		}
85		rl := strings.ToLower(rationale)
86		matches := 0
87		seen := map[string]bool{}
88		for _, t := range tokenizeRationale(acceptance) {
89			if seen[t] {
90				continue
91			}
92			seen[t] = true
93			if strings.Contains(rl, t) {
94				matches++
95				if matches >= 2 {
96					return true
97				}
98			}
99		}
100		return false
101	}
102	
103	// tokenizeRationale extracts lower-cased alphanumeric tokens of length ≥ 6
104	// for the AC-overlap check. The threshold is higher than the AC-coverage
105	// metric in triage.go because a rationale citing AC items typically reuses
106	// a noun or verb from the AC ("test", "rate", "limit" are too generic).
107	func tokenizeRationale(s string) []string {
108		var tokens []string
109		var cur strings.Builder
110		for _, r := range strings.ToLower(s) {
111			if (r >= 'a' && r <= 'z') || (r >= '0' && r <= '9') {
112				cur.WriteRune(r)
113			} else {
114				if cur.Len() >= 6 {
115					tokens = append(tokens, cur.String())
116				}
117				cur.Reset()
118			}
119		}
120		if cur.Len() >= 6 {
121			tokens = append(tokens, cur.String())
122		}
123		return tokens
124	}
125	
126	// AttemptFunc runs one execute-bead attempt and returns its report.
127	type AttemptFunc func(ctx context.Context) (ExecuteBeadReport, error)
128	
129	// SleepFunc abstracts time.Sleep so tests can inject a fake clock.
130	type SleepFunc func(ctx context.Context, d time.Duration) error
131	
132	// DefaultSleep is a context-aware time.Sleep used as the default SleepFunc.
133	func DefaultSleep(ctx context.Context, d time.Duration) error {
134		if d <= 0 {
135			return nil
136		}
137		timer := time.NewTimer(d)
138		defer timer.Stop()
139		select {
140		case <-ctx.Done():
141			return ctx.Err()
142		case <-timer.C:
143			return nil
144		}
145	}
146	
147	// LockBackoffResult reports the outcome of RunWithLockBackoff.
148	type LockBackoffResult struct {
149		// Report is the final attempt's report (success or last failed retry).
150		Report ExecuteBeadReport
151		// Err is the final attempt's error, if any.
152		Err error
153		// Retries is the number of retry attempts performed *after* the initial
154		// run. 0 means the first run did not see a lock-contention error.
155		Retries int
156		// Exhausted is true when every retry slot in the backoff schedule was
157		// consumed and the final attempt still reported lock_contention. The
158		// caller should escalate via the triage policy.
159		Exhausted bool
160		// SleepDurations records each backoff that was waited, in order. Tests
161		// assert on this to verify the schedule was honoured.
162		SleepDurations []time.Duration
163	}
164	
165	// RunWithLockBackoff invokes attempt and, when it returns a lock-contention
166	// failure, retries up to len(backoff) times with the matching backoff between
167	// attempts. Returns as soon as an attempt produces any non-lock-contention
168	// outcome (success or other failure). When every retry is consumed and the
169	// final attempt is still lock_contention, Exhausted is true.
170	func RunWithLockBackoff(ctx context.Context, attempt AttemptFunc, backoff []time.Duration, sleep SleepFunc) LockBackoffResult {
171		if sleep == nil {
172			sleep = DefaultSleep
173		}
174		res := LockBackoffResult{}
175		report, err := attempt(ctx)
176		res.Report = report
177		res.Err = err
178		for i, d := range backoff {
179			if !attemptIsLockContention(res.Report, res.Err) {
180				return res
181			}
182			if serr := sleep(ctx, d); serr != nil {
183				// Ctx cancelled mid-backoff: surface ctx error, exhaust flag stays
184				// false because we did not finish the schedule.
185				res.Err = serr
186				return res
187			}
188			res.SleepDurations = append(res.SleepDurations, d)
189			report, err = attempt(ctx)
190			res.Report = report
191			res.Err = err
192			res.Retries = i + 1
193		}
194		if attemptIsLockContention(res.Report, res.Err) {
195			res.Exhausted = true
196		}
197		return res
198	}
199	
200	func attemptIsLockContention(report ExecuteBeadReport, err error) bool {
201		if err != nil && IsLockContentionError(err.Error()) {
202			return true
203		}
204		if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed && IsLockContentionError(report.Detail) {
205			return true
206		}
207		return false
208	}
209	
210	// TriageDispatchStore is the subset of bead.Store used by the dispatcher.
211	// *bead.Store satisfies it (compile-time check below).
212	type TriageDispatchStore interface {
213		Create(b *bead.Bead) error
214		Update(id string, mutate func(*bead.Bead)) error
215		AppendEvent(id string, event bead.BeadEvent) error
216		Events(id string) ([]bead.BeadEvent, error)
217		CloseWithEvidence(id, sessionID, commitSHA string) error
218	}
219	
220	// HistoryFromEvents extracts the ordered list of prior triage failure modes
221	// for a bead from its event log. The dispatcher records each decision as a
222	// `triage-decision` event whose body carries `mode=<failure_mode>`. Older
223	// decisions appear first.
224	func HistoryFromEvents(events []bead.BeadEvent) []triage.FailureMode {
225		var hist []triage.FailureMode
226		for _, ev := range events {
227			if ev.Kind != "triage-decision" {
228				continue
229			}
230			mode := extractTriageEventField(ev.Body, "mode")
231			if mode == "" {
232				continue
233			}
234			hist = append(hist, triage.FailureMode(mode))
235		}
236		return hist
237	}
238	
239	func extractTriageEventField(body, key string) string {
240		prefix := key + "="
241		for _, line := range strings.Split(body, "\n") {
242			line = strings.TrimSpace(line)
243			if strings.HasPrefix(line, prefix) {
244				return strings.TrimSpace(strings.TrimPrefix(line, prefix))
245			}
246		}
247		return ""
248	}
249	
250	// DispatchInput carries the per-attempt context for a triage decision.
251	type DispatchInput struct {
252		Bead      bead.Bead
253		Mode      triage.FailureMode
254		Rationale string // for no_changes
255		SessionID string // for closing the parent on file_followup
256		BaseRev   string
257		Now       func() time.Time
258	}
259	
260	// DispatchOutcome is the result of DispatchPostAttempt. Action is what the
261	// triage policy returned; FollowupID is set when ActionFileFollowup created
262	// a child bead. ParentClosed is true when the original bead was closed as
263	// part of the followup-file action.
264	type DispatchOutcome struct {
265		Action       triage.Action
266		FollowupID   string
267		ParentClosed bool
268	}
269	
270	// DispatchPostAttempt is the central entry point invoked by the loop after
271	// an attempt classifies into a triage-tracked failure mode. It records a
272	// `triage-decision` event with the chosen action and, when the action calls
273	// for it, files a clarification follow-up bead and closes the parent.
274	//
275	// Lock-contention retry is NOT performed here — callers wrap the executor
276	// with RunWithLockBackoff before invoking DispatchPostAttempt. By the time
277	// this function runs the in-pass retry schedule is already exhausted.
278	func DispatchPostAttempt(store TriageDispatchStore, policy triage.TriagePolicy, in DispatchInput) (DispatchOutcome, error) {
279		if in.Now == nil {
280			in.Now = time.Now
281		}
282		events, err := store.Events(in.Bead.ID)
283		if err != nil {
284			return DispatchOutcome{}, fmt.Errorf("triage dispatch: read events: %w", err)
285		}
286		history := HistoryFromEvents(events)
287		action := policy.Decide(in.Bead.ID, in.Mode, history)
288	
289		out := DispatchOutcome{Action: action}
290	
291		// Record the triage decision event before any further side effects so
292		// future Decide() calls see the advance even if the followup-file path
293		// fails partway through.
294		body, _ := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
295			"mode":      string(in.Mode),
296			"action":    string(action),
297			"history":   history,
298			"rationale": in.Rationale,
299		})
300		bodyStr := fmt.Sprintf("mode=%s\naction=%s\n\n%s", in.Mode, action, string(body))
301		if err := store.AppendEvent(in.Bead.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
302			Kind:      "triage-decision",
303			Summary:   fmt.Sprintf("%s → %s", in.Mode, action),
304			Body:      bodyStr,
305			Actor:     "ddx",
306			Source:    "ddx triage dispatch",
307			CreatedAt: in.Now().UTC(),
308		}); err != nil {
309			return out, fmt.Errorf("triage dispatch: append event: %w", err)
310		}
311	
312		if action == triage.ActionFileFollowup && in.Mode == triage.FailureModeNoChanges {
313			childID, ferr := fileNoChangesFollowup(store, in)
314			if ferr != nil {
315				return out, ferr
316			}
317			out.FollowupID = childID
318			// Close the parent: the no_changes outcome means the agent claims
319			// nothing to do, but the rationale is too weak to call the AC met.
320			// The follow-up bead carries the clarification work; this one is
321			// closed without a commit (BaseRev is the closing SHA).
322			closingSHA := in.BaseRev
323			if err := store.CloseWithEvidence(in.Bead.ID, in.SessionID, closingSHA); err != nil {
324				return out, fmt.Errorf("triage dispatch: close parent: %w", err)
325			}
326			out.ParentClosed = true
327		}
328		return out, nil
329	}
330	
331	// fileNoChangesFollowup creates a child clarification bead. The child
332	// inherits the parent's spec-id and labels, and its description quotes the
333	// weak rationale and the parent AC so a future attempt has enough context
334	// to either redo the work or confirm satisfaction.
335	func fileNoChangesFollowup(store TriageDispatchStore, in DispatchInput) (string, error) {
336		parent := in.Bead
337		rationale := strings.TrimSpace(in.Rationale)
338		if rationale == "" {
339			rationale = "(agent provided no rationale)"
340		}
341	
342		desc := fmt.Sprintf(
343			"Clarify acceptance criteria for parent bead %s.\n\n"+
344				"The agent reported no_changes with a rationale that does not cite the parent's acceptance criteria. "+
345				"The original bead has been closed; this follow-up captures the unresolved question so a human or "+
346				"a stronger model can either confirm satisfaction with concrete evidence or do the work.\n\n"+
347				"Worker rationale:\n%s\n\n"+
348				"Parent acceptance criteria:\n%s",
349			parent.ID, rationale, strings.TrimSpace(parent.Acceptance))
350	
351		acceptance := fmt.Sprintf(
352			"1. Confirm whether each AC item from %s is genuinely satisfied. "+
353				"For each item, either cite a commit SHA / test name proving it, or do the work and commit it. "+
354				"2. If satisfied, close with evidence; if not, the implementation lands here.",
355			parent.ID)
356	
357		labels := append([]string{}, parent.Labels...)
358		if !HasBeadLabel(labels, "kind:clarification") {
359			labels = append(labels, "kind:clarification")
360		}
361	
362		extra := map[string]any{
363			"triage_followup_for":     parent.ID,
364			"triage_followup_reason":  "no_changes_weak_rationale",
365			"triage_parent_rationale": rationale,
366		}
367		if parent.Extra != nil {
368			if specID, ok := parent.Extra["spec-id"]; ok && specID != "" {
369				extra["spec-id"] = specID
370			}
371		}
372	
373		child := &bead.Bead{
374			Title:       "Clarify: " + parent.Title,
375			Description: desc,
376			Acceptance:  acceptance,
377			Labels:      labels,
378			Parent:      parent.ID,
379			Extra:       extra,
380		}
381		if err := store.Create(child); err != nil {
382			return "", fmt.Errorf("triage dispatch: file followup bead: %w", err)
383		}
384	
385		// Cross-link the parent so the closure carries a pointer to the
386		// followup bead's id. AppendEvent on the parent is best-effort; failure
387		// here does not abort the dispatch because the child is already filed.
388		body, _ := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
389			"followup_id": child.ID,
390			"reason":      "no_changes_weak_rationale",
391			"rationale":   rationale,
392		})
393		_ = store.AppendEvent(parent.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
394			Kind:      "triage-followup-filed",
395			Summary:   fmt.Sprintf("filed clarification follow-up %s", child.ID),
396			Body:      string(body),
397			Actor:     "ddx",
398			Source:    "ddx triage dispatch",
399			CreatedAt: in.Now().UTC(),
400		})
401		return child.ID, nil
402	}
403	
404	// Compile-time check: bead.Store satisfies TriageDispatchStore.
405	var _ TriageDispatchStore = (*bead.Store)(nil)
406

1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"errors"
6		"testing"
7		"time"
8	
9		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
10		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/triage"
11		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
12		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
13	)
14	
15	func TestIsLockContentionError(t *testing.T) {
16		cases := []struct {
17			msg  string
18			want bool
19		}{
20			{"Unable to create '.git/index.lock': File exists.", true},
21			{"fatal: another git process seems to be running in this repository", true},
22			{".git/index.lock: file exists", true},
23			{"staging-tracker lock held by another process", true},
24			{"tracker lock held: try again later", true},
25			{"build failed: undefined: foo.Bar", false},
26			{"401 unauthorized", false},
27			{"", false},
28		}
29		for _, tc := range cases {
30			t.Run(tc.msg, func(t *testing.T) {
31				if got := IsLockContentionError(tc.msg); got != tc.want {
32					t.Fatalf("IsLockContentionError(%q)=%v want %v", tc.msg, got, tc.want)
33				}
34			})
35		}
36	}
37	
38	func TestClassifyAttemptForTriage(t *testing.T) {
39		cases := []struct {
40			name string
41			rep  ExecuteBeadReport
42			want triage.FailureMode
43		}{
44			{"review_block",
45				ExecuteBeadReport{Status: ExecuteBeadStatusReviewBlock},
46				triage.FailureModeReviewBlock},
47			{"no_changes",
48				ExecuteBeadReport{Status: ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges},
49				triage.FailureModeNoChanges},
50			{"execution_failed_lock",
51				ExecuteBeadReport{Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
52					Detail: "Unable to create '.git/index.lock'"},
53				triage.FailureModeLockContention},
54			{"execution_failed_other",
55				ExecuteBeadReport{Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
56					Detail: "agent crashed"},
57				triage.FailureModeExecutionFailed},
58			{"success",
59				ExecuteBeadReport{Status: ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess},
60				triage.FailureMode("")},
61		}
62		for _, tc := range cases {
63			t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
64				got := ClassifyAttemptForTriage(tc.rep)
65				if got != tc.want {
66					t.Fatalf("got %q want %q", got, tc.want)
67				}
68			})
69		}
70	}
71	
72	func TestRationaleCitesAC(t *testing.T) {
73		ac := "1. The retry-with-backoff dispatcher must escalate after exhaustion. " +
74			"2. The TriagePolicy ladder must advance per attempt."
75		cases := []struct {
76			name      string
77			rationale string
78			want      bool
79		}{
80			{"empty", "", false},
81			{"vague", "nothing to do here", false},
82			{"cites two AC tokens",
83				"the dispatcher already has retry-with-backoff and the triagepolicy ladder is in place; see TestX",
84				true},
85			{"cites SHA",
86				"already done in 4fde2b25, see commit log", true},
87			{"cites Go test name",
88				"see TestRetryWithBackoff in dispatcher_test.go", true},
89			{"only one AC token",
90				"backoff was implemented", false},
91		}
92		for _, tc := range cases {
93			t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
94				got := RationaleCitesAC(tc.rationale, ac)
95				if got != tc.want {
96					t.Fatalf("RationaleCitesAC(%q)=%v want %v", tc.rationale, got, tc.want)
97				}
98			})
99		}
100	}
101	
102	// TestRunWithLockBackoffSucceedsOnRetry — AC#4: lock-error retry success.
103	// First attempt fails with .git/index.lock; the dispatcher waits the first
104	// backoff slot and the second attempt succeeds. The schedule's later slots
105	// are not consumed.
106	func TestRunWithLockBackoffSucceedsOnRetry(t *testing.T) {
107		calls := 0
108		attempt := func(ctx context.Context) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
109			calls++
110			if calls == 1 {
111				return ExecuteBeadReport{
112					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
113					Detail: "Unable to create '.git/index.lock': File exists.",
114				}, nil
115			}
116			return ExecuteBeadReport{Status: ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, ResultRev: "abc"}, nil
117		}
118		var slept []time.Duration
119		sleep := func(_ context.Context, d time.Duration) error {
120			slept = append(slept, d)
121			return nil
122		}
123		backoff := []time.Duration{30 * time.Second, 90 * time.Second, 300 * time.Second}
124	
125		res := RunWithLockBackoff(context.Background(), attempt, backoff, sleep)
126	
127		assert.Equal(t, 2, calls, "second attempt must succeed")
128		assert.Equal(t, 1, res.Retries, "exactly one retry consumed")
129		assert.False(t, res.Exhausted)
130		assert.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, res.Report.Status)
131		assert.Equal(t, []time.Duration{30 * time.Second}, slept,
132			"only the first backoff slot was consumed")
133	}
134	
135	// TestRunWithLockBackoffExhausts — AC#4: lock-error backoff exhaustion.
136	// Three retry slots, four lock-contention attempts in total → exhausted.
137	// All three backoff slots are slept in order before final escalation.
138	func TestRunWithLockBackoffExhausts(t *testing.T) {
139		attempt := func(ctx context.Context) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
140			return ExecuteBeadReport{
141				Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
142				Detail: "Unable to create '.git/index.lock': File exists.",
143			}, nil
144		}
145		var slept []time.Duration
146		sleep := func(_ context.Context, d time.Duration) error {
147			slept = append(slept, d)
148			return nil
149		}
150		backoff := []time.Duration{30 * time.Second, 90 * time.Second, 300 * time.Second}
151	
152		res := RunWithLockBackoff(context.Background(), attempt, backoff, sleep)
153	
154		assert.Equal(t, 3, res.Retries, "three retries against the schedule")
155		assert.True(t, res.Exhausted, "schedule must be exhausted")
156		assert.Equal(t, backoff, slept, "all three backoff slots consumed in order")
157		assert.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, res.Report.Status)
158	}
159	
160	// TestRunWithLockBackoffPropagatesNonLockError — non-lock errors must NOT
161	// trigger retry. The dispatcher must return immediately with the first
162	// attempt's outcome.
163	func TestRunWithLockBackoffPropagatesNonLockError(t *testing.T) {
164		calls := 0
165		attempt := func(ctx context.Context) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
166			calls++
167			return ExecuteBeadReport{
168				Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
169				Detail: "401 unauthorized",
170			}, nil
171		}
172		res := RunWithLockBackoff(context.Background(), attempt, LockContentionBackoff,
173			func(_ context.Context, _ time.Duration) error { return nil })
174		assert.Equal(t, 1, calls)
175		assert.Equal(t, 0, res.Retries)
176		assert.False(t, res.Exhausted)
177		assert.Empty(t, res.SleepDurations)
178	}
179	
180	// TestRunWithLockBackoffRetriesOnAttemptError — when the AttemptFunc itself
181	// returns an error whose text matches the lock pattern, the dispatcher
182	// should retry too. This covers callers who surface lock contention as a
183	// returned error rather than a populated report.
184	func TestRunWithLockBackoffRetriesOnAttemptError(t *testing.T) {
185		calls := 0
186		attempt := func(ctx context.Context) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
187			calls++
188			if calls == 1 {
189				return ExecuteBeadReport{}, errors.New("staging-tracker lock held: retry")
190			}
191			return ExecuteBeadReport{Status: ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess}, nil
192		}
193		res := RunWithLockBackoff(context.Background(), attempt,
194			[]time.Duration{1 * time.Millisecond},
195			func(_ context.Context, _ time.Duration) error { return nil })
196		assert.Equal(t, 2, calls)
197		assert.Equal(t, 1, res.Retries)
198		assert.False(t, res.Exhausted)
199	}
200	
201	// TestDispatchExecutionFailedEscalates — AC#4: execution_failed escalation.
202	// First failure → re_attempt_with_context. Second → escalate_tier. Third
203	// → needs_human. Each call records a triage-decision event so future Decide()
204	// calls see the prior history.
205	func TestDispatchExecutionFailedEscalates(t *testing.T) {
206		store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
207		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
208		parent := &bead.Bead{Title: "Failing thing", Acceptance: "AC text"}
209		require.NoError(t, store.Create(parent))
210	
211		policy := triage.DefaultPolicy()
212		now := func() time.Time { return time.Date(2026, 5, 2, 12, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC) }
213	
214		dispatch := func() triage.Action {
215			b, err := store.Get(parent.ID)
216			require.NoError(t, err)
217			out, err := DispatchPostAttempt(store, policy, DispatchInput{
218				Bead: *b,
219				Mode: triage.FailureModeExecutionFailed,
220				Now:  now,
221			})
222			require.NoError(t, err)
223			return out.Action
224		}
225	
226		wantLadder := []triage.Action{
227			triage.ActionReAttemptWithContext,
228			triage.ActionEscalateTier,
229			triage.ActionNeedsHuman,
230			triage.ActionNeedsHuman, // clamps to last rung
231		}
232		for i, want := range wantLadder {
233			got := dispatch()
234			if got != want {
235				t.Fatalf("attempt %d: got %q want %q", i+1, got, want)
236			}
237		}
238	
239		// The ladder advances were materialised as triage-decision events.
240		events, err := store.Events(parent.ID)
241		require.NoError(t, err)
242		var decisions []bead.BeadEvent
243		for _, ev := range events {
244			if ev.Kind == "triage-decision" {
245				decisions = append(decisions, ev)
246			}
247		}
248		assert.Len(t, decisions, len(wantLadder))
249	
250		// HistoryFromEvents recovers the modes in order.
251		hist := HistoryFromEvents(events)
252		for _, m := range hist {
253			assert.Equal(t, triage.FailureModeExecutionFailed, m)
254		}
255	}
256	
257	// TestDispatchNoChangesFilesFollowup — AC#4: no_changes follow-up.
258	// When the worker rationale does not cite any AC items, the policy returns
259	// file_followup on the second occurrence; the dispatcher creates a child
260	// clarification bead, links the parent, and closes the parent.
261	func TestDispatchNoChangesFilesFollowup(t *testing.T) {
262		store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
263		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
264		parent := &bead.Bead{
265			Title:      "Implement the gizmo",
266			Acceptance: "1. The frobnicator must validate inputs. 2. Add a regression test for invalid power.",
267		}
268		require.NoError(t, store.Create(parent))
269	
270		policy := triage.DefaultPolicy()
271		now := func() time.Time { return time.Date(2026, 5, 2, 12, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC) }
272	
273		// First no_changes attempt: rationale is weak. Default policy ladder rung 1
274		// is re_attempt_with_context — no follow-up is filed yet, but a triage
275		// decision is recorded so the next call sees the advance.
276		pCopy, _ := store.Get(parent.ID)
277		out, err := DispatchPostAttempt(store, policy, DispatchInput{
278			Bead:      *pCopy,
279			Mode:      triage.FailureModeNoChanges,
280			Rationale: "nothing left to do here",
281			Now:       now,
282		})
283		require.NoError(t, err)
284		assert.Equal(t, triage.ActionReAttemptWithContext, out.Action)
285		assert.Empty(t, out.FollowupID)
286		assert.False(t, out.ParentClosed)
287	
288		// Second no_changes: ladder advances to file_followup. Dispatcher files a
289		// clarification child and closes the parent.
290		pCopy, _ = store.Get(parent.ID)
291		out, err = DispatchPostAttempt(store, policy, DispatchInput{
292			Bead:      *pCopy,
293			Mode:      triage.FailureModeNoChanges,
294			Rationale: "nothing to do, really",
295			SessionID: "sess-1",
296			BaseRev:   "abc1234",
297			Now:       now,
298		})
299		require.NoError(t, err)
300		assert.Equal(t, triage.ActionFileFollowup, out.Action)
301		require.NotEmpty(t, out.FollowupID, "follow-up bead must be created")
302		assert.True(t, out.ParentClosed, "parent must be closed")
303	
304		// Parent is closed.
305		got, err := store.Get(parent.ID)
306		require.NoError(t, err)
307		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusClosed, got.Status)
308	
309		// Child has parent link + clarification labels + back-reference in Extra.
310		child, err := store.Get(out.FollowupID)
311		require.NoError(t, err)
312		assert.Equal(t, parent.ID, child.Parent)
313		assert.True(t, HasBeadLabel(child.Labels, "kind:clarification"))
314		assert.Equal(t, parent.ID, child.Extra["triage_followup_for"])
315		assert.Equal(t, "no_changes_weak_rationale", child.Extra["triage_followup_reason"])
316		assert.Contains(t, child.Description, "nothing to do, really")
317		assert.Contains(t, child.Acceptance, parent.ID)
318	
319		// triage-followup-filed event references the child.
320		events, err := store.Events(parent.ID)
321		require.NoError(t, err)
322		var followupEv *bead.BeadEvent
323		for i := range events {
324			if events[i].Kind == "triage-followup-filed" {
325				followupEv = &events[i]
326			}
327		}
328		require.NotNil(t, followupEv, "triage-followup-filed event missing")
329		assert.Contains(t, followupEv.Body, out.FollowupID)
330	}
331	
332	// TestDispatchNoChangesStrongRationaleStaysOnLadder — when the rationale
333	// cites the AC, this signals the bead is genuinely satisfied; the existing
334	// adjudicateNoChanges path closes it as already_satisfied. The dispatcher
335	// must NOT file a clarification follow-up in that case. We verify by
336	// ensuring callers can keep the dispatcher off the path: the dispatcher only
337	// fires when the caller has decided weak rationale → triage. When
338	// RationaleCitesAC returns true, the caller should skip DispatchPostAttempt
339	// for no_changes entirely. This unit test just confirms RationaleCitesAC
340	// returns true on a strong rationale containing an AC token.
341	func TestDispatchNoChangesStrongRationaleSkipsTriage(t *testing.T) {
342		ac := "1. The retry-with-backoff dispatcher must escalate after exhaustion."
343		rationale := "Already implemented: see retry-with-backoff in TestRunWithLockBackoffExhausts; commit 4fde2b25."
344		if !RationaleCitesAC(rationale, ac) {
345			t.Fatalf("strong rationale should be recognised as citing AC")
346		}
347	}
348

commit e188d9d328252379837926e4c7c50f001e9f805c
Author: Test User <test@example.com>
Date:   Sat May 2 09:38:55 2026 -0400

    feat(agent): wire triage dispatch for lock_contention/execution_failed/no_changes [.execute-bead-wt-ddx-3c154349-20260502T131050-bf2d17f5-2b64cb0a]
    
    Adds the post-attempt triage integration described by the parent bead's AC:
    
    - IsLockContentionError + ClassifyAttemptForTriage map .git/index.lock and
      staging-tracker lock errors to triage.FailureModeLockContention; other
      execution_failed details fall through to FailureModeExecutionFailed; the
      worker-level FailureModeLockContention constant is added to ClassifyFailureMode.
    - RunWithLockBackoff retries an AttemptFunc against a 3-step backoff schedule
      (default 30s/90s/300s); succeeds on first non-lock outcome, returns
      Exhausted=true after the schedule is consumed so callers can escalate via
      the policy.
    - DispatchPostAttempt records a triage-decision event per attempt, advancing
      the policy ladder; HistoryFromEvents recovers prior modes from the event
      log so re-entry counts stay correct across drain passes.
    - For ActionFileFollowup on no_changes, fileNoChangesFollowup creates a
      clarification child bead with the parent's AC text and the weak rationale,
      then closes the parent. RationaleCitesAC gates the path so strong
      rationales (cites SHA, Go test name, or share 2+ AC tokens) stay on the
      already_satisfied path.
    
    Tests cover all four AC items: lock-error retry success on second attempt;
    lock-error backoff exhaustion through three slots; execution_failed laddering
    through re_attempt -> escalate_tier -> needs_human; no_changes weak rationale
    filing a follow-up and closing the parent.

input.content:
no_changes: bead is already implemented in the current base.

The work described by this bead's AC was implemented and merged in commit
e188d9d3 ("feat(agent): wire triage dispatch for
lock_contention/execution_failed/no_changes [...]"), which is an ancestor of
the current execute-bead base 26d80c83. That commit added 755 lines across
three files:

  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go    (+3)
  cli/internal/agent/triage_dispatch.go        (+405)
  cli/internal/agent/triage_dispatch_test.go   (+347)

AC mapping (all satisfied by code already present in the worktree):

  AC#1 — Lock-contention classification + retry-with-backoff with ≥3 steps
    before escalation:
    - IsLockContentionError (triage_dispatch.go) matches .git/index.lock,
      "another git process seems to be running", staging-tracker / tracker
      lock messages.
    - ClassifyAttemptForTriage routes execution_failed reports whose Detail
      matches IsLockContentionError to triage.FailureModeLockContention.
    - RunWithLockBackoff retries an AttemptFunc against a backoff schedule;
      LockContentionBackoff defaults to {30s, 90s, 300s} (three slots),
      sets Exhausted=true after consuming all slots so callers escalate.
    - DefaultPolicy.Ladders[FailureModeLockContention] =
      {retry_with_backoff, retry_with_backoff, needs_human} so cross-pass
      lock_contention also escalates.

  AC#2 — execution_failed enters triage and produces escalate_tier or
    needs_human depending on attempt count:
    - DefaultPolicy.Ladders[FailureModeExecutionFailed] =
      {re_attempt_with_context, escalate_tier, needs_human}.
    - DispatchPostAttempt records a triage-decision event per attempt;
      HistoryFromEvents recovers the prior-mode count so the ladder
      advances per occurrence (verified by
      TestDispatchExecutionFailedEscalates).

  AC#3 — no_changes with rationale not citing AC items files a clarification
    follow-up and closes the original:
    - RationaleCitesAC returns false on vague rationales and true when the
      rationale (a) is "specific" via rationaleIsSpecific (cites SHA, test
      name, file path) or (b) shares ≥2 AC content tokens of length ≥6.
    - DispatchPostAttempt, on ActionFileFollowup for FailureModeNoChanges,
      calls fileNoChangesFollowup which creates a child bead inheriting
      labels/spec-id, adds kind:clarification, embeds the worker's
      rationale and parent AC, and back-links via the
      triage_followup_for / triage_followup_reason Extra fields. Then
      CloseWithEvidence closes the parent (BaseRev as closing SHA).

  AC#4 — Tests cover all four scenarios:
    - TestRunWithLockBackoffSucceedsOnRetry — lock-error retry success
    - TestRunWithLockBackoffExhausts — backoff exhaustion across all three
      schedule slots
    - TestDispatchExecutionFailedEscalates — full execution_failed ladder
      (re_attempt → escalate_tier → needs_human, then clamps)
    - TestDispatchNoChangesFilesFollowup — weak-rationale no_changes files
      a clarification child and closes the parent
    Plus TestIsLockContentionError, TestClassifyAttemptForTriage,
    TestRationaleCitesAC, TestRunWithLockBackoffPropagatesNonLockError,
    TestRunWithLockBackoffRetriesOnAttemptError,
    TestDispatchNoChangesStrongRationaleSkipsTriage as supporting cases.

Verification:
  cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/ -run \
    'TestRunWithLockBackoff|TestDispatch|TestClassifyAttemptForTriage|TestIsLockContentionError|TestRationaleCitesAC' \
    -count=1
  → ok  github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent  0.024s
